items.title: Kakadu locals call for support following outstation floods

children.content: Kakadu residents whose homes have been submerged by roof-high floodwater said they feel like they've been forgotten, with evacuation centres and relief payments not made available to them.

image.altText: House in back left of image under water, halfway up its front porch, flood waters, small green trees in foreground.

items.title: ASIC expands in NT after report into Indigenous customers' high bank fees

children.content: The chair of the Australian Securities and Investments Commission says the expansion of the watchdog agency in Darwin will help the organisation better enforce regulations in remote communities.

image.altText: Shoulder-up shot of a white man, gray hair, wearing light blue collared shirt, light-framed glasses, neutral expression

items.title: Katherine's worst flood in 28 years leaves survivors with disaster 'PTSD'

children.content: As it recovers from its worst flooding in decades, those living in the NT town of Katherine are trying to come to terms with the ongoing threat on their doorstep.

articles.title: Katherine escapes more flood damage as remote evacuees start heading home

children.content: Major flooding has eased along the Katherine River and the town has been spared further damage, while surrounding remote communities are starting to recover.

image.altText: People stand near a large fallen tree.

gallery.altText: A man stands next to a fence covered in mud.
A ute sits in floodwaters after being washed off a road.

thumbnail.altText: A man stands next to a fence covered in mud.
A ute sits in floodwaters after being washed off a road.

items.title: Senior NT Police officer demoted 'because of race', lawsuit claims

children.content: A former NT Police Acting Assistant Commissioner has claimed his career is in tatters after he was overlooked for promotion and demoted for "reasons including" his race.

image.altText: A police officer in a police uniform speaks at a press conference

items.title: Four cases of potentially deadly strain of diphtheria confirmed in NT

children.content: The Northern Territory's health department has declared an outbreak of respiratory diphtheria in the Top End.

image.altText: A medical professional wearing gloves and holding a syringe

items.title: Trial of two men accused of rape in Darwin hears closing arguments

children.content: The jury in the trial of two men accused of picking up a young woman waiting for an Uber after a night out and raping her in her home has heard closing submissions.

image.altText: Close ups of both men

items.title: 25yo Indigenous man dies in NT police custody after detainment in car cage

children.content: A 25-year-old Indigenous man has died in police custody in Darwin's northern suburbs overnight, with police saying he was "arrested without incident".

image.altText: The driveway into the village is shielded by black bar gates

items.title: PM says he's in contact with South-East Asian partners over fuel security — as it happened

children.content: The prime minister says he is in contact with South-East Asian partners over fuel security as the war in the Middle East continues.

image.altText: The prime minister looks at journalists.

items.title: Is the end of the Iran war near?

children.content: Donald Trump says Iran wants a ceasefire deal. But Tehran says it's not even talking with the US. So what's going on and how likely is a ceasefire?

image.altText: Donald Trump looking up in an inquisitive manner

items.title: ASX drops following sea of red on Wall St

children.content: The ASX falls at the open, following a sea of red on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: The indicator board at the Australian Stock Exchange displaying red

items.title: 'I've got a family to support': Sandilands's battle to stay on air in court

children.content: Kyle Sandilands says he wants to "get back to work as quick as possible" as a legal battle between the controversial radio host and his former employer reaches court for the first time.

image.altText: Kyle Sandilands has white hair and a beard and is surrounded by reporters.

items.title: Superannuation giant HESTA's new admin provider on brink of collapse

children.content: Experts are concerned one of Australia's largest super funds has invested in a new admin company whose most recent financial report indicates it is insolvent.

image.altText: HESTA logo on a wall

items.title: Big Red Bash cancelled amid site change and rising fuel prices

children.content: This festival usually sees people go all out, spending thousands of dollars and travelling thousands of kilometres, but a potential change in location and fuel concerns have deterred ticket holders.

image.altText: photo of stage and crowd at Big Red Bash

items.title: Nazeem Hussain developed his 'gut-punch' comedy over a lifetime of racism

children.content: For comedian Nazeem Hussain, the best way to respond to war, discrimination and racism is to laugh about it. And he's built his whole career on this special brand of risky, outrageous comedy.

gallery.altText: Actor Nazeem Hussain stands smiling on a beach at sunset
Nazeem Hussain and Virginia Trioli sit at a cafe table smiling, papers and photos in front of them
Nazeem Hussain on stage with big lights behind him, holding a microphone and smiling

Nazeem Hussain on stage with big lights behind him, holding a microphone and smiling

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Warnings of abuse as trans women banned from Olympic female events

children.content: Under changes announced by the International Olympic Committee, the gender of athletes will be determined by a one-time gene-screening test.

image.altText: Weightlifter Laurel Hubbard drops the bar behind herself as she competes in front of the Olympic rings.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Jet fuel shortages, headbutting whales and Donald Trump's social faux pas

children.content: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

items.title: Grey's Anatomy marks 21 years of love, loss and disasters

children.content: Twenty-one years ago today, a group of interns walked into the fictional Seattle Grace Hospital to start their surgical careers. It was the start of the longest-running medical drama in US TV history.

image.altText: A woman in blue scrubs and a white coat looking concerned.

items.title: 'Reliable evidence': Exercise halves early death risk of middle-aged women

children.content: Australian researchers found that middle-aged women who exercised regularly halved their risk of premature death. But most women aren't meeting physical activity guidelines.

image.altText: Woman jogging while listening music at beach against sky

items.title: Parts of NSW wake up to 'surreal' snowfall in March

children.content: It is still only March, but snow is falling in parts of NSW and the Bureau of Meteorology is checking its record books.

gallery.altText: A man in a green coat and red pants standing in snow with a tree in the background.
Scenic landscape with light dusting of snow

thumbnail.altText: A man in a green coat and red pants standing in snow with a tree in the background.
Scenic landscape with light dusting of snow

items.title: Verstappen kicks Guardian F1 reporter out of Japanese GP press conference

children.content: Max Verstappen refuses to speak to media until Guardian journalist Giles Richards leaves because of a question he asked last year.

image.altText: Max Verstappen talks to a reporter.

items.title: Why millions are boycotting AI as debate rages over its ethical use

children.content: AI companies are stuck in a "perfect storm" of trying to control the use and output of their platforms while maintaining competitive profits, as millions of people boycott the technology, experts say.

image.altText: Male protesters walking in a group while holding 'Stop the AI Race' placards with red stop signs over 'AI'.

items.title: Where is Gus? Six months on, more questions than answers remain

children.content: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

image.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

items.title: Balcony identified as likely mould infection site linked to patient deaths

children.content: A balcony at the Royal Prince Alfred Hospital has been identified as the infection site of a mould cluster linked to two patient deaths late last year.

image.altText: An aerial shot of construction works red arrow pointing to top of a building

items.title: Craig's experience of abuse is not uncommon — but it is seldom talked about

children.content: Craig Hughes-Cashmore spent more than 20 years living with persistent suicidal thoughts, an experience that many share but rarely feel able to discuss.

items.title: Assault charges against Wendell Sailor dismissed on mental health grounds

children.content: All charges are dropped against former footballer Wendell Sailor, who is discharged into his doctor's care.

image.altText: Man in dark suit, pink shirt and grey hat

items.title: Conflict reshapes ancient festival for Iranian Australians

children.content: As Iranian Australians try to celebrate Persian new year, news of the conflict in the Middle East is always front of mind.

image.altText: Leila looking at the camera with a backdrop of cultural artworks in the background at Wagga's library.

items.title: Agencies warn aged support services face cuts as fuel prices soar

children.content: The national peak body for aged care providers is calling on the federal government to provide urgent support to keep services running, as the Middle East war sends fuel prices skyrocketing.

image.altText: images of a hospital like setting with aged care beds

items.title: Petition calls for video of death in custody to be released despite coroner ruling

children.content: Raylene Nixon says the public should be able to make their own minds up about what happened to her son in his final moments.

image.altText: Man in yellow shirt with cap stands in front of Aboriginal painting.

items.title: Country town tackles energy uncertainty by building its own solar farm

children.content: Hundreds of residents of a rural NSW town combine to build their own commercial solar farm, demonstrating the community benefits of renewable energy.

gallery.altText: Family of six sitting and smailing amongst a solar farm

thumbnail.altText: Family of six sitting and smailing amongst a solar farm

items.title: More than 1,000 Australians physically assaulted every day, new data reveals

children.content: Almost 400,000 Australians experienced physical assault in 2024-2025, according to new victimisation data released by the Australian Bureau of Statistics.

image.altText: An image of a clenched fist.

items.title: More than 250 voters in Coober Pedy unable to have their say in SA election

children.content: Nearly one in three Coober Pedy voters, or more than 250 people, missed their chance to vote in the state election last week after the town was left without a polling booth on election day.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Coober Pedy

items.title: Complaints about foul smells skyrocket in Melbourne's west

children.content: Adam Pattison spent two years doing up his backyard to enjoy outdoor barbecues, but says "sickening" smells kill the vibe five times a week.

image.altText: Adam Pattison looking annoyed in his backyard

items.title: How 'traditional' gender roles are making a comeback in modern society

children.content: It turns out generation Z has some of the strongest traditional beliefs about gender roles. The revival of what many see as outdated gender norms is being attributed to "manosphere" content on social media.

image.altText: A family of two children, a man and a woman at a table. The left half is black and white; the right is red and white.

items.title: How Australians interact with birds and wildlife around their homes

children.content: Our recent stories about attracting native fauna to gardens and feeding wild birds resonated strongly. Here is how some Australians interact with wildlife in their local areas.

gallery.altText: A black and white magpie sits on a small branch, looking at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white magpie sits on a small branch, looking at the camera.

avatar.alt: Portrait of journalist Daniel Johnson wearing a grey and maroon plaid shirt and crossing his arms.
